unitTitle: Unit 2: Past simple, continuous & used to

content.subtitle: Use
items: Actions or events finished in the past
Things that happened again and again in the past
Situations that existed in the past but no longer do
The key events that move a story forward

content.subtitle: Examples
items: We **moved** to a new house last spring.
As a child, she **visited** her grandparents every Sunday.
People **didn't use** smartphones thirty years ago.
He **grabbed** his bag, **ran** down the stairs and **jumped** into the car.

content.subtitle: Useful notes
items: yesterday
last week/summer/year/etc
in January/2001/etc
an hour/a week/a year ago

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: Some verbs have irregular past simple forms.

content.subtitle: Use
items: Describing what was happening at a specific time in the past
Two activities going on simultaneously in the past
Setting the scene at the start of a story

content.subtitle: Examples
items: At eight o'clock this morning, Dad **was making** breakfast.
She **was talking** on the phone while her brother **was playing** video games.
The sun **was shining** and the birds **were singing** — it was a perfect day.

content.subtitle: Useful notes
items: at that moment
at one/two/etc o'clock
while

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: When a shorter action interrupts a longer one in the past, combine the past simple and past continuous: I **dropped** my keys while I **was running** to the bus.
Do not use the past continuous for regular or repeated actions in the past.

content.subtitle: Use
items: Past habits or states that no longer exist

content.subtitle: Example
items: My grandfather **used to** walk five kilometres to school when he was young.

sections.title: A: Past simple (verbs in the box)
sections.instructions: Complete using the correct past simple form of the verbs in the box. You may have to use some negative forms.

items.sentence: I got to the post office just before it closed and ...... the letter.
We invited Stephanie to the party, but she ...... .
Jack lost his job because he ...... too many mistakes.
Everyone ...... that it was Bill's fault, but nobody said anything.
Karen ...... the keys from the kitchen table and ran out the door.
I was bored, so Mum ...... me some money to go shopping.
Do you remember the time we ...... to India on holiday?
It started raining, but luckily I ...... an umbrella in my bag.

sections.title: B: Past simple from picture prompts
sections.instructions: Look at the pictures and complete the sentences. Use the correct form of the past simple.

items.sentence: I don't want to go and see the film because I ...... .
I don't need a football because ...... .
I know a lot about Paris because ...... .
items.answer: I went there two years ago

items.sentence: I don't need to worry about my homework because ...... .
I haven't got a PlayStation any more because ...... .
Mum is angry with me because ...... .
items.answer: I broke the window a week ago

sections.title: C: Past continuous
sections.instructions: Complete using the correct past continuous form of the verbs in brackets.

items.sentence: Ted ...... (play) his guitar at half past seven.
At midnight, I ...... (sleep), but Jane ...... (listen) to music.
Luke ...... (stand) outside the bank when suddenly two robbers ran past him.
I know Doug ...... (work) late at the office because I saw him when I ...... (leave).
...... you ...... (have) a shower when the earthquake happened?
Penny ...... (run) to catch the bus when she slipped and fell.
When you saw Eugene, ...... he ...... (go) home?
At midnight? Erm ... we ...... (watch) a DVD, I think.

sections.title: D: Circle the correct word or phrase
sections.instructions: Circle the correct word or phrase.

items.sentence: When we were in Canada, we **went / were going** skiing almost every day.
About four years ago, I **decided / was deciding** to become a chef.
Georgia **had / was having** a shower when someone knocked at the door.
Holly and I ran from the house to the taxi because it **rained / was raining** heavily.
Two men **argued / were arguing** outside, so I went to see what was happening.
Daniel **called / was calling** you at one o'clock yesterday, but you were here with me.
We **ate / were eating** breakfast when a letter came through the letter box.
As I walked past the window, I saw that Paula **made / was making** a cake.
I **dreamt / was dreaming** about my favourite band when the alarm clock went off.
While I **practised / was practising** the trumpet late last night, a neighbour came to complain.

sections.title: E: Amber's Big Match
sections.instructions: Complete using the correct past simple or past continuous form of the verbs in the box.

items.sentence:
One morning, Amber (1) ...... up early. The sun (2) ...... and the birds (3) ...... . Amber (4) ...... very excited because it was the day of the big tennis match.
Amber (5) ...... downstairs and into the kitchen, where her father (6) ...... breakfast.
'Morning, Amber. Today's the day!' he (7) ...... . Amber smiled nervously. 'Don't worry!' he (8) ...... . 'You'll be fine.'
Amber (9) ...... some toast into the toaster and (10) ...... the fridge. Just as she (11) ...... the butter out, the phone (12) ...... .
Her father (13) ...... it. After a few minutes, he put the phone down.
'Bad news, I'm afraid. The other player (14) ...... yesterday when she had an accident. The match is off.'
Amber ate her toast slowly. She was surprised she didn't feel disappointed.

sections.title: F: used to
sections.instructions: Complete using the correct form of used to. You may have to use some negative forms.

items.sentence: When I was younger, I ...... eat pizza almost every day!
...... there ...... be a supermarket on the corner?
Bradley is a teacher, but he ...... want to be a train driver.
I ...... like eating cabbage, but now I love it!
...... Rick ...... have blond hair when he was a little boy?
I know Lily ...... cook much, but now I think she makes dinner every day.
